climate.description: Late August in eastern Norway offers mild summer weather with long daylight.

fondo_rating.scoring_notes: Migrated from gravel-race-automation. Original score: 73. adventure dimension dropped, road_surface added. Score recalculated: 74.

course_description.character: IMPORTANT — this profile describes a road edition that no longer exists. LandeveisBirken, the road-cycling pilot, ran point-to-point from Rena to Lillehammer for exactly three editions: 2012, 2013, and 2014. Distances varied by year (roughly 160-171 km on the main course), and the route was not fully paved — contemporary reports describe a gravel sector of roughly 7 km on Militærvegen plus rough secondary-road surfaces despite the 'road' positioning. Birken AS discontinued it after the 2014 pilot, saying it hadn't developed as hoped and didn't fit the core Birken concept. The backpack tradition and Raudfjellet/Midtfjellet references previously in this profile describe the CURRENT Birkebeinerrittet MTB race, a separate, still-active event that is mountain-bike/gravel, not road — it should not be presented as a road cycling option.

logistics.lodging_strategy: Hotels and mattress accommodations in Rena, Lillehammer, or Hamar; book via My Page post-registration.
logistics.transport: Start bib pickup in Rena on race morning; buses from Oslo/Lillehammer to Rena; finish at Håkons Hall with shuttles back.

history.origin_story: Annual MTB classic since 1993 commemorating Birkebeiner warriors' 1206 ski rescue of heir Håkon Håkonsson; world's largest by entrants.

biased_opinion.summary: There is no current road edition of Birkebeinerrittet to rate. LandeveisBirken, the road-cycling pilot version, ran for three editions (2012-2014) before Birken AS discontinued it, saying the concept hadn't developed as hoped and didn't fit the traditional Birken identity of mountains, terrain, and the historic backpack. The 2012 launch sold out its 1,500-rider allocation, but by 2014 the main event drew only 696 registered riders — a real decline that preceded the shutdown. Today's Birkebeinerrittet family (Birkebeinerrittet MTB, HalvBirken, UltraBirken, GravelBirken) is entirely off-road; the organizer has explicitly removed drop-bar/AllRoad categories and directs road-curious riders to GravelBirken instead.
weaknesses: This event does not currently exist — it should not be presented to riders as a bookable road race
Even during its three-year run, it wasn't fully paved: contemporary reports describe a rough gravel sector (~7 km on Militærvegen) and uneven secondary roads despite its road-event branding
Participation declined significantly from its sold-out 2012 launch to 2014, ahead of discontinuation
biased_opinion.bottom_line: Retired. If you want a Birkebeinerrittet-family event today, it's MTB (Birkebeinerrittet MTB) or gravel (GravelBirken) — there is no road option, and hasn't been since 2014.

final_verdict.one_liner: Norway's backpacked MTB legend: history, mountains, and massive community.
final_verdict.should_you_race: No — this road edition does not exist. If you want the Birkebeinerrittet experience, look at the current Birkebeinerrittet MTB or GravelBirken events instead.
final_verdict.alternatives: Birkebeinerrittet MTB (current mountain-bike edition, birkebeiner.no) or GravelBirken (current gravel-bike edition) — both active, neither is road cycling.

citations.label: Birken AS Confirms End of Three-Year LandeveisBirken Road Pilot

catalog_flags.status_note: defunct road edition (2012-2014); course_description was corrected this wave to the actual defunct road-pilot facts, but race.name/display_name ('Birkebeinerrittet Sykkel') and vitals (84km/1,215m/Aug 29) still describe the CURRENT, still-active MTB event, not the defunct road pilot — recommend a human decide whether this profile stays in the road catalog at all (Roadie Labs is a road-cycling database and no road version currently exists), and if kept, rename/re-vital it to the actual road-pilot data (LandeveisBirken, ~160-171km Rena-Lillehammer, disputed elevation)

eligibility.notes: LandeveisBirken road pilot 2012-2014 discontinued by Birken AS; current lineup is MTB/gravel only — our Aug 29 date belongs to the MTB race
